feature: Remove wildcard default from github_environments to enforce environment-based access control
workflow_rationale: Single Terraform module security fix with minimal file changes and no multi-service dependencies. This is a straightforward variable default change with validation.

phases.name: Security Fix Implementation
phases.description: Remove wildcard default from github_environments variable and add validation to prevent wildcards

subtasks.description: Remove wildcard default from github_environments variable and add validation
patterns_from: variables.tf (lines 56-59 show wildcard validation pattern for repo variable)
subtasks.implementation_notes: Change github_environments default from ['*'] to [] (empty list). Add validation block to reject wildcards, similar to the repo variable validation pattern. This is a BREAKING CHANGE.

subtasks.description: Update README.md documentation to reflect breaking change

verification.instructions: Review README.md to ensure: 1) github_environments default is updated from ['*'] to [], 2) Security rationale is explained in a BREAKING CHANGE section, 3) Migration guidance is provided for existing users

subtasks.implementation_notes: Add BREAKING CHANGE notice explaining that wildcard environments are no longer permitted. Update the inputs table to show new default value. Provide migration guidance.

phases.name: Final Verification
phases.description: Verify Terraform formatting and validation pass, and documentation is complete

subtasks.description: Run terraform fmt and validate on the entire module

verification.expected: All files formatted correctly and validation passes

subtasks.implementation_notes: This ensures all Terraform files are properly formatted and syntactically correct after the changes.

acceptance_criteria: github_environments variable no longer defaults to ['*']
Validation prevents wildcard values in github_environments
README.md documents the breaking change with migration guidance

verification_steps.name: Documentation Review
verification_steps.expected_outcome: README.md clearly explains breaking change and provides migration guidance

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk because this is a BREAKING CHANGE that affects all users relying on the wildcard default. However, it's a necessary security fix. No unit tests exist for Terraform modules, so validation relies on terraform fmt and validate commands.

checks: Variable validation blocks are syntactically correct
README.md updated with new github_environments default value
BREAKING CHANGE section added with security rationale
Migration guidance provided for existing users
Inputs table reflects accurate default value
